Mac: "zsh: command not found: xxx" — Two Common Causes and Fixes
is one of the most common errors people hit on Mac, so I'm writing down how I solve it.
You don't use nvm — you installed Node.js directly
Both trigger the same error on zsh — any global command fails:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

zsh: command not found: hexo
zsh: command not found: vue-cli
...
Most dev setups use nvm to manage Node.js versions — fast switching, isolated package sets. But sometimes you'll get:
If you're lucky, you'll see this in the terminal:

nvm is not compatible with the npm config "prefix" option: currently set to "/Users/ray/.npm-global"
Run `nvm use --delete-prefix v14.15.1 --silent` to unset it.
nvm is not compatible with the npm config "prefix" option: currently set to "/Users/ray/.npm-global"
Run `nvm use --delete-prefix v14.15.1 --silent` to unset it.
That message is gold — it tells you exactly what's wrong. Your npm prefix is set somewhere nvm doesn't manage, so nvm's bin paths aren't being reached. Fixing it:
Replace xx.xx.xx with your Node version. If you're using v14.15.1:
This switches to that Node version and removes the conflicting npm prefix, which lets nvm work properly.
Try running a global command now — if it works, you're done. If not, keep going.

This wipes the stored prefix so npm falls back to defaults.

After that, the "command not found" issue is resolved and the
nvm is not compatible with the npm config "prefix"
itself works fine but globally-installed packages throw "command not found", your zsh PATH isn't picking up npm's global bin directory. Fix it manually.
Confirm the path to your zsh config:
Open it and check for the npm bin path:
If it's there, your PATH config is fine — possible cause is npm cache:
Step 3: Manually add the npm bin path
If the export line isn't there, add it:
This appends npm's global bin path to your zsh PATH.
Either way — restart your terminal or reload the config so the change takes effect:
What causes these in the first place? On nvm — usually a Node.js version update where npm's prefix didn't get updated cleanly, leaving nvm and npm pointing at different places.
Without nvm — usually because npm's global bin path was never added to zsh's PATH, often due to permission issues during install or someone just forgetting to set it.
Permission can also be a culprit if you literally can't install global packages — try
for the install. Otherwise read the actual error to figure out what's wrong.
nvm is not compatible with the npm config "prefix" option

Hit "zsh: command not found: hexo" or similar on Mac after installing a global npm package? Two flavors of this issue, both common — one if you use nvm (npm prefix conflicting with nvm), one if you don't (zsh PATH not picking up npm's global bin directory). This post covers both fixes step by step.
